question: Identify the state that fits both descriptions: among sponsors behind independently funded political advertising and related election-season messages in Washington, no more than 10 sponsors from that state used at least 25 different vendors; and in February 2011, the state's average household electricity price across ZIP codes for bundled service was at least $0.025 per kWh higher than its average business price. As of 2025, among electricity-generating power stations in that state, which station had the greatest capacity in megawatts? Your response should be in the format [Answer].

1.fact:
import io
import pandas as pd
import boto3
from botocore import UNSIGNED
from botocore.config import Config

source = "datagov/independent-campaign-expenditures-and-electioneering-communications/files/rows.txt"
bucket = "lakeqa-yc4103-datalake"
obj = boto3.client("s3", config=Config(signature_version=UNSIGNED)).get_object(Bucket=bucket, Key=source)
df = pd.read_csv(io.BytesIO(obj["Body"].read()), low_memory=False)

state_map = {
    "AL": "Alabama", "AK": "Alaska", "AZ": "Arizona", "AR": "Arkansas", "CA": "California",
    "CO": "Colorado", "CT": "Connecticut", "DE": "Delaware", "FL": "Florida", "GA": "Georgia",
    "HI": "Hawaii", "ID": "Idaho", "IL": "Illinois", "IN": "Indiana", "IA": "Iowa",
    "KS": "Kansas", "KY": "Kentucky", "LA": "Louisiana", "ME": "Maine", "MD": "Maryland",
    "MA": "Massachusetts", "MI": "Michigan", "MN": "Minnesota", "MS": "Mississippi", "MO": "Missouri",
    "MT": "Montana", "NE": "Nebraska", "NV": "Nevada", "NH": "New Hampshire", "NJ": "New Jersey",
    "NM": "New Mexico", "NY": "New York", "NC": "North Carolina", "ND": "North Dakota", "OH": "Ohio",
    "OK": "Oklahoma", "OR": "Oregon", "PA": "Pennsylvania", "RI": "Rhode Island", "SC": "South Carolina",
    "SD": "South Dakota", "TN": "Tennessee", "TX": "Texas", "UT": "Utah", "VT": "Vermont",
    "VA": "Virginia", "WA": "Washington", "WV": "West Virginia", "WI": "Wisconsin", "WY": "Wyoming",
    "DC": "District of Columbia"
}

filtered = df[
    df["sponsor_state"].notna()
    & df["sponsor_name"].notna()
    & df["vendor_name"].notna()
    & df["expenditure_amount"].notna()
].copy()
filtered["expenditure_amount"] = pd.to_numeric(filtered["expenditure_amount"], errors="coerce")
filtered = filtered[filtered["expenditure_amount"].notna()].copy()
filtered["state"] = filtered["sponsor_state"].map(state_map).fillna(filtered["sponsor_state"])

result = (
    filtered.groupby("state", dropna=False)
    .agg(
        sponsor_count=("sponsor_name", "nunique"),
        vendor_count=("vendor_name", "nunique")
    )
    .reset_index()
)

result = result[(result["vendor_count"] >= 25) & (result["sponsor_count"] <= 10)]
result = result.sort_values("state").reset_index(drop=True)
answer = result["state"].tolist()
1.subquestion: Which states were home to sponsors behind independently funded political advertising and related election-season messages in Washington when no more than 10 sponsors from the state used at least 25 different vendors?

2.fact:
import io
import pandas as pd
import boto3
from botocore import UNSIGNED
from botocore.config import Config

source = "datagov/u-s-electric-utility-companies-and-rates-look-up-by-zipcode-feb-2011-a2fc8/files/iouzipcodes2011.txt"
bucket = "lakeqa-yc4103-datalake"
obj = boto3.client("s3", config=Config(signature_version=UNSIGNED)).get_object(Bucket=bucket, Key=source)
df = pd.read_csv(io.BytesIO(obj["Body"].read()), low_memory=False)

state_map = {
    "AL": "Alabama", "AK": "Alaska", "AZ": "Arizona", "AR": "Arkansas", "CA": "California",
    "CO": "Colorado", "CT": "Connecticut", "DE": "Delaware", "FL": "Florida", "GA": "Georgia",
    "HI": "Hawaii", "ID": "Idaho", "IL": "Illinois", "IN": "Indiana", "IA": "Iowa",
    "KS": "Kansas", "KY": "Kentucky", "LA": "Louisiana", "ME": "Maine", "MD": "Maryland",
    "MA": "Massachusetts", "MI": "Michigan", "MN": "Minnesota", "MS": "Mississippi", "MO": "Missouri",
    "MT": "Montana", "NE": "Nebraska", "NV": "Nevada", "NH": "New Hampshire", "NJ": "New Jersey",
    "NM": "New Mexico", "NY": "New York", "NC": "North Carolina", "ND": "North Dakota", "OH": "Ohio",
    "OK": "Oklahoma", "OR": "Oregon", "PA": "Pennsylvania", "RI": "Rhode Island", "SC": "South Carolina",
    "SD": "South Dakota", "TN": "Tennessee", "TX": "Texas", "UT": "Utah", "VT": "Vermont",
    "VA": "Virginia", "WA": "Washington", "WV": "West Virginia", "WI": "Wisconsin", "WY": "Wyoming",
    "DC": "District of Columbia"
}

filtered = df[
    (df["service_type"] == "Bundled")
    & df["state"].notna()
    & df["res_rate"].notna()
    & df["comm_rate"].notna()
].copy()
filtered["res_rate"] = pd.to_numeric(filtered["res_rate"], errors="coerce")
filtered["comm_rate"] = pd.to_numeric(filtered["comm_rate"], errors="coerce")
filtered = filtered[filtered["res_rate"].notna() & filtered["comm_rate"].notna()].copy()
filtered["state_full"] = filtered["state"].map(state_map).fillna(filtered["state"])

result = (
    filtered.groupby("state_full", dropna=False)
    .agg(
        avg_comm=("comm_rate", "mean"),
        avg_res=("res_rate", "mean")
    )
    .reset_index()
)
result["res_comm_gap"] = result["avg_res"] - result["avg_comm"]
result = result[result["res_comm_gap"] >= 0.025].sort_values("state_full").reset_index(drop=True)
answer = result["state_full"].tolist()
2.subquestion: In February 2011, which states had average household electricity prices across ZIP codes for bundled service that were at least $0.025 per kWh higher than the corresponding average business prices?

3.subquestion: As of 2025, among electricity-generating power stations in <hop 1 answer>, which station had the greatest capacity in megawatts?

reasoning: Node 1: Filter rows where `sponsor_state`, `sponsor_name`, `vendor_name`, and `expenditure_amount` are present, convert `expenditure_amount` to numeric, normalize `sponsor_state` values to full state names, aggregate by state to compute distinct sponsor and vendor counts, and keep states with at least 25 distinct vendors and no more than 10 distinct sponsors.

Node 2: Filter rows where `service_type` = `Bundled`, `state` is present, and both `res_rate` and `comm_rate` are present, convert `res_rate` and `comm_rate` to numeric, normalize `state` values to full state names, aggregate by state to compute average commercial and average residential rates, compute `res_comm_gap = avg_res - avg_comm`, and keep states with `res_comm_gap >= 0.025`.
Hop result: semantic intersection of <node 1 answer> and <node 2 answer> = Virginia.

Node 3: Use <hop 1 answer> as the state. In the 2025 source snapshot, compare listed capacities for Virginia power stations: Bath County Pumped Storage Station is 3,030 MW, ahead of North Anna Nuclear Generating Station at 1,790 MW, Brunswick County at 1,472 MW, and Chesterfield Power Station at 1,415 MW.
Hop result: as of 2025, Bath County Pumped Storage Station had the greatest listed capacity among electricity-generating power stations in Virginia.
